title: 10 best herbal tinctures for dermatitis

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle tinctures helps with dermatitis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can soothe itchy skin and reduce redness. The tincture's antihistamine effects also help to alleviate itching and irritation caused by allergic reactions or environmental factors. Additionally, stinging nettle's antioxidant properties can promote healthy skin cell growth and tissue repair, making it an effective natural remedy for dermatitis sufferers looking to reduce their reliance on harsh chemicals and topical creams.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Nettle tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and sterols, help reduce inflammation and alleviate itching and swelling associated with dermatitis.

Antioxidant properties: Netlle tincture's rich antioxidant content helps neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate skin conditions like dermatitis, reducing oxidative stress and promoting healthy skin cell function.

Immunomodulatory effects: Nettle tincture's immunomodulatory compounds, such as polyacetylenes, help regulate the immune system's response to allergens and irritants, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with dermatitis.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids (specifically, histamine-releasing alkaloids): These compounds help reduce inflammation and itching associated with dermatitis by mimicking the body's natural response to allergens.

Phenolic acids (specifically, caffeic acid and ferulic acid): These antioxidants help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that contribute to dermatitis symptoms.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin): These plant-derived compounds have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help alleviate itching, redness, and swelling associated with dermatitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties to reduce itching and inflammation associated with dermatitis.
Stems: Utilized for their similar properties to leaves, providing relief from dermatitis symptoms.
Roots: Employed for their ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, making them a popular choice for dermatitis tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh urtica dioica leaves and stems in early morning when they are at their highest potency for medicine.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces using sharp scissors or a sharp knife for ease of extraction.
Combine the chopped plant material with a neutral solvent like 80 proof vodka or glycerin in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar daily to enhance extraction.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to remove plant material.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with dermatitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The tannins and flavonoids present in pot marigold extract have been shown to reduce inflammation and itching associated with skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. Additionally, the antimicrobial compounds in pot marigold help to combat bacterial and fungal infections that can exacerbate skin symptoms, promoting a healthy balance of the skin's natural microbiome and reducing the risk of flare-ups.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Pot marigold tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation and itching associated with dermatitis.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant properties of pot marigold tincture help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate dermatitis symptoms.

Wound-healing and soothing effects: Pot marigold tincture has been traditionally used to promote wound healing and soothe irritated skin, making it an effective treatment for dermatitis-related skin lesions and rashes.
remedy_constituents: Sesquiterpene lactones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with dermatitis.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Calendula, such as triterpenoid saponins, have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals.

Carotenoids (including beta-carotene): Carotenoids in Calendula have antioxidant properties, which help to protect the skin from oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting healing and reducing the severity of dermatitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help soothe and heal skin irritations.
Leaves: Used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making them effective in treating dermatitis symptoms.
Stems: Not as commonly used, but some herbal remedies might use the stems to create a tincture that aids in reducing inflammation and promoting skin health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh flowers in their prime when the petals are fully open and the centres are yellow and dry.
Dry the flowers in a warm well-ventilated area for 2 weeks to a moisture content of 10 percent.
Use a 1 part herb to 2 parts solvent ratio to make 1000ml of tincture with 25 percent solvent volume.
Steep the dried flowers in the solvent at room temperature for 2 to 6 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter into a clean dark glass bottle and store it in a cool place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with dermatitis because of its unique blend of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and soothing properties. The herb's ability to calm irritated skin and reduce inflammation makes it an effective natural remedy for alleviating the symptoms of dermatitis, such as itching, redness, and swelling. Additionally, the tincture's antimicrobial properties help to combat bacterial infections that can exacerbate the condition, promoting healing and relief from discomfort. As a result, herbal Kansas coneflower tinctures offer a holistic approach to managing dermatitis and promoting healthy skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Kansas coneflower's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with dermatitis.

Antioxidant properties: The tannins and phenolic acids present in Kansas coneflower tinctures have antioxidant effects, which help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress that can exacerbate dermatitis.

Immunomodulatory properties: Kansas coneflower's triterpenoids and sesquiterpenes may modulate the immune system to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of dermatitis by inhibiting pro-inflammatory cytokines and promoting anti-inflammatory ones.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with dermatitis.
Alkaloids: Echinacea angustifolia contains alkaloids that exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help soothe and calm irritated skin and reduce inflammation.
Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates have immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune response and reduce the severity of dermatitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are the primary part used for their high concentration of alkaloids and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating properties beneficial for skin conditions.
Leaves: Leaves are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the skin in cases of dermatitis.
Flowers: Flowers are used for their ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making them beneficial for skin conditions like dermatitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea angustifolia roots in late summer or early fall when the plants are in full bloom.
Clean and chop the roots into small pieces to increase the surface area for extraction.
Combine 1 part chopped roots with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and let it steep for 2 to 6 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort tinctures helps with dermatitis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which work together to soothe and calm irritated skin. The herb has been traditionally used to treat various skin conditions, including eczema, psoriasis, and rosacea. Its active compounds, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have been shown to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with dermatitis, promoting a sense of comfort and relief for those affected.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: St John's Wort contains compounds like hyperforin and adhyperforin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with dermatitis such as redness, swelling, and itching.

Antioxidant properties: The tincture's high antioxidant content helps protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate conditions like dermatitis and promote collagen breakdown, leading to premature aging and skin thickening.

Immunomodulatory properties: St John's Wort has been shown to modulate the immune response, which is important in conditions like dermatitis where an overactive or underactive immune system can contribute to inflammation and skin damage.
remedy_constituents: Naphthodianthrones: These compounds, particularly hyperforin and hypericin, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with dermatitis.

Flavonoids (e.g., quercetin, kaempferol): These polyphenolic compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of dermatitis such as itching, redness, and skin irritation.

Phenolic acids (e.g., ferulic acid, caffeic acid): These compounds exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help protect the skin from oxidative stress and reduce the severity of dermatitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties useful in treating dermatitis.

Flowers: Rich in hyperforin and hypericin, which have shown to have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antihistamine properties, making them effective in soothing skin irritations and reducing inflammation.

Roots: The roots contain a high amount of hypericin, which has shown to have antiseptic, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating various skin conditions, including dermatitis.
remedy_recipe: Gather 25 grams of fresh or dried hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves in a clean glass jar.
Combine the gathered plant material with 50 percent vodka or glycerin in a 1:2 ratio by volume.
Steep the mixture for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place away from direct sunlight.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Store the final tincture in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with dermatitis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tannins present in dandelion roots have been shown to reduce inflammation and itching, while the flavonoids help to soothe and calm irritated skin. Additionally, dandelion's diuretic properties can aid in reducing water retention, which can contribute to skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. As a natural remedy, dandelion tinctures provide a gentle yet effective solution for managing symptoms of dermatitis, promoting healthy and balanced skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with dermatitis.

Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's antioxidant properties can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to skin damage and oxidative stress, reducing the severity of dermatitis symptoms like redness and itching.

Digestive support: Dandelion tincture also supports digestive health by stimulating bile production and improving gut motility, which can alleviate digestive issues that often accompany dermatitis and reduce inflammation in the skin.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-derived compounds help alleviate dermatitis by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which are common contributing factors to skin irritation and inflammation.
Taraxasterol: A triterpene saponin found in dandelion, Taraxasterol has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in soothing and protecting skin affected by dermatitis.

Kaempferol: A type of flavonoid, Kaempferol is known for its strong anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce skin inflammation, itching, and redness associated with dermatitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxeryl acetate, which help soothe and calm skin irritations.
Roots: Contain inulin and other polysaccharides that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, reducing skin inflammation and infection.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, promoting skin healing and reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-50 fresh taraxacum officinale roots and clean them thoroughly with cool water to remove dirt.
Chop the cleaned taraxacum officinale roots into small pieces and measure out a 1:2 root-to-solvent ratio.
Combine the chopped taraxacum officinale pieces with 100 proof vodka in a clean glass jar to create a mixture.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks to allow for maceration and extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids after 2-3 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with dermatitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The tincture's active compounds, such as apigenin and chamazulene, penetrate deep into the skin to calm irritated skin tissues and reduce redness and swelling. By reducing inflammation, chamomile tinctures can alleviate itching, burning sensations, and discomfort associated with dermatitis. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties help to prevent infections and promote healthy skin healing. Overall, chamomile tinctures provide a natural and effective solution for relieving dermatitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with dermatitis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.

Antioxidant property: Chamomile's antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to dermatitis symptoms, promoting overall skin health and soothing irritation.

Soothing and calming property: Chamomile's volatile oils, particularly bisabolol, have a gentle, anti-irritant effect on the skin, providing rapid relief from itching, burning, and stinging sensations common in dermatitis.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing redness and swelling associated with dermatitis.

Furanocoumarins (e.g., bergamottin, bergapten): These compounds have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties, which can help alleviate the itching and discomfort associated with dermatitis.
Luteolin: This flavonoid exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to protect the skin from damage and reduce the severity of dermatitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Matricaria chamomilla are used for their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to calm skin irritations and reduce inflammation associated with dermatitis.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce skin inflammation and promote wound healing in cases of dermatitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of matricaria chamomilla flowers on a dry sunny day after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested flowers in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours to preserve their potency.
Combine 250g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with 750ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 6 weeks shaking the jar every day to facilitate extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and store the tincture in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tinctures helps with dermatitis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The herb's allantoin content reduces redness and swelling, while its flavonoids and triterpenoid saponins work to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate the condition. Additionally, comfrey's ability to stimulate collagen production helps improve skin elasticity, reducing the appearance of scars and promoting overall skin health. As a result, herbal comfrey tinctures provide natural relief from dermatitis symptoms, leaving skin feeling smooth, calm, and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Wound-healing and tissue regeneration: Comfrey's ability to stimulate collagen synthesis, improve blood flow, and accelerate wound closure helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing in dermatitis-prone skin.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects: Comfrey's tannins, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to dermatitis symptoms such as redness, itching, and swelling.

Soothing and protective properties: Comfrey's mucilages and saponins provide a gentle, non-irritating coating on the skin, helping to soothe and protect irritated skin, reduce scratching, and promote a healthy barrier function.
remedy_constituents: Allantoin: It helps with dermatitis by promoting wound healing, reducing inflammation, and accelerating the regeneration of skin cells.

Symphytine (a complex of phenolic compounds): It has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and swelling associated with dermatitis.
Triterpenoid saponins: They exhibit anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help to reduce the severity of dermatitis symptoms, such as itching and skin lesions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Anti-inflammatory properties in the leaves help soothe and calm skin irritations associated with dermatitis.
Roots: Rich in antioxidants and bioactive compounds in the roots aid in reducing inflammation and promoting skin healing.
Stems: The stems of Symphytum officinale contain mucilages, which provide soothing and protective effects for skin affected by dermatitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh symphytum officinale leaves and flowers at peak potency to ensure optimal tincture quality.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces to increase the surface area for efficient extraction.
Combine the chopped plant material with 500 milliliters of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar to create the extraction mixture.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate the extraction process.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container, discard the solids and store the tincture in a cool dark place.

Anti-inflammatory activity: The tincture contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the skin, providing relief from discomfort associated with dermatitis.

Barks: They are used for their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce redness and itching caused by dermatitis.

remedy_recipe: Harvest tilia platyphyllos flowers and leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated and the sun is not intense.
Clean the harvested tilia platyphyllos plant material and dry it thoroughly for 7 to 10 days.
Combine 1 part of the dried tilia platyphyllos plant material with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a dark cool place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container and discard the solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems and dry them for 2-3 weeks.
Chop the dried gaultheria procumbens into small pieces and measure out 1 part of the material.
Combine the chopped gaultheria procumbens with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass container.
Store the container in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks to allow extraction to occur.

remedy_recipe: Gather baptisia tinctoria roots and flowers in bulk quantities for tincture production purposes.
Chop 100 grams of the roots and flowers into small pieces for easier extraction.
Combine the chopped baptisia tinctoria material with 500 milliliters of 40% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks shaking occasionally.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container discard the solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has dried ensuring freshness.
Dry the plant material using shade or low-temperature drying methods within 24 hours of harvest.
Grind 100 grams of dried plant material into a fine powder and combine with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka.
Steep the mixture for 14 days in a cool dark place shaking the container daily to ensure thorough infusion.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding solids after completion.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright tinctures helps with dermatitis because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried euphrasia officinalis leaves and stems in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Weigh out 500 grams of euphrasia officinalis material and chop it into small pieces using scissors or a knife.
Combine chopped plant material with 1000 milliliters of vodka at 40% strength in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks shaking the jar twice daily for eight minutes.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding solids and labeling the final product.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with dermatitis because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal extract contains mucilages, which form a protective barrier on the skin's surface, reducing redness and itching associated with dermatitis. remedy_properties: Soothing anti-inflammatory properties: Marshmallow tincture's mucilage content helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate itching, redness, and swelling associated with dermatitis.

Antioxidant and antimicrobial properties: Marshmallow tincture's antioxidant properties help to combat free radicals that can exacerbate dermatitis, while its antimicrobial properties prevent bacterial infections and promote wound healing.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried marshmallow root, ensuring it meets pharmaceutical standards quality.
Clean and chop the marshmallow root into small pieces, approximately one-quarter inch in size, to enhance extraction.
Combine the chopped marshmallow root with 80-proof vodka or everclear, using a ratio of one part herb to five parts solvent, in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for six to eight weeks, shaking the jar daily, to allow for maximum bioavailability and solubility.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and storing the liquid in a cool dark place.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried taraxacum kok-saghyz roots in early morning, cutting them into small pieces and rinsing with cool water.
Combine the chopped root material with 40% vodka in a clean glass jar, leaving about an inch of headspace at the top.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking the mixture daily during this time period.
After the maceration process is complete, strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass bottle, discarding any solids.
Store the final taraxacum kok-saghyz tincture in an amber-colored bottle in the refrigerator, using it within six months and dosing 20-30 drops per serving as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with dermatitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The antioxidants present in lavender, particularly luteolin and apigenin, help to reduce redness and swelling associated with skin irritations. Additionally, the antiseptic properties of lavender work to prevent infection and promote healing of affected areas. This natural remedy soothes itching and discomfort, allowing the skin to recover from inflammation and irritation caused by dermatitis. Regular use of English lavender tincture can help alleviate symptoms and promote long-term skin health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lavender's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with dermatitis, providing relief from uncomfortable symptoms.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties in lavender tincture help protect the skin from free radical damage caused by environmental stressors and oxidative stress, which can contribute to skin conditions like dermatitis.

Soothing and calming: Lavender's natural sedative and calming effects can promote relaxation, reducing stress and anxiety that can exacerbate dermatitis symptoms and promoting a healthy healing environment for the skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are often used in herbal medicine due to their high concentration of essential oils, which can help soothe and calm skin irritations associated with dermatitis.
Flowers: Lavender flowers have been traditionally used to create topical creams and ointments that alleviate symptoms of eczema, a common condition that can cause dermatitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried lavender flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine one part of the powdered lavender with two parts of vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the liquid mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Transfer the resulting tincture to dark glass bottles and store them in the refrigerator, using within one year after preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with dermatitis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe itchy skin and reduce redness. The tannins present in lemon balm also help to calm down skin irritation, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving the discomfort associated with dermatitis. Additionally, lemon balm's antimicrobial properties can aid in preventing bacterial infections that often accompany dermatitis, promoting a faster recovery. Overall, herbal lemon balm tinctures offer a gentle and non-invasive approach to managing dermatitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Lemon balm's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to skin conditions like dermatitis.
Anti-inflammatory effects: The flavonoids present in lemon balm have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate itching, redness, and swelling associated with dermatitis.

Calming and soothing effects: Lemon balm's sedative and anti-anxiety properties can help calm the skin and reduce stress-related symptoms of dermatitis, promoting a more balanced and healthy skin environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon balm roots have been traditionally used to treat skin issues, including dermatitis, due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Leaves: The leaves of the lemon balm plant contain compounds like citral and geraniol, which have been shown to have antipruritic and anti-itching effects, making them useful for soothing itchy skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried melissa officinalis leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves using a clean cloth or paper towels within an hour of harvesting.
Chop the cleaned melissa officinalis leaves into small pieces and combine them with 40-50% vodka or high-proof neutral grain spirit.
Store the mixture in a dark glass bottle and allow it to steep for 2 weeks, shaking the bottle daily, before straining and discarding solids.
Filter the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean dark glass bottle and store it in the refrigerator for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena tinctures helps with dermatitis because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The herb's flavonoids and phenolic acids work together to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with skin conditions like eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis. Additionally, lemon verbena's calming effects help soothe irritated skin, promoting a healthy balance of the skin's natural barrier function. By addressing these underlying issues, herbal lemon verbena tinctures can provide effective relief from painful and uncomfortable skin symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon verbena's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as verbascoside and luteolin, help to reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with dermatitis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory molecules.

Antioxidant properties: The rich antioxidant content in lemon verbena tinctures helps to neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to the development of dermatitis, thereby protecting the skin from oxidative stress.

Soothing and calming properties: Lemon verbena's calming effects on the skin are attributed to its ability to reduce irritation, itching, and discomfort associated with dermatitis by exerting a sedative effect on nerve endings.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemon verbena roots are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, making them effective in soothing skin irritations and reducing inflammation associated with dermatitis.
Seeds: Lemon verbena seeds contain essential fatty acids that help to moisturize the skin, reduce dryness and itchiness, and promote healing in dermatitis-prone areas.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried verbena officinalis leaves and flowers in a clean glass jar.
Combine 250ml of vodka with 30g of chopped herb material in the glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another glass container, discarding solids.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store at room temperature for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder tinctures helps with dermatitis because of their potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tannins present in elder flowers have been shown to reduce inflammation and itching associated with dermatitis, providing relief for irritated skin. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of elder tinctures can help combat underlying bacterial or fungal infections that may exacerbate the condition. As a natural alternative to harsh topical creams, elder tinctures offer a gentle and soothing approach to managing dermatitis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Elder tincture's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the skin, which can contribute to the development and exacerbation of dermatitis.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in elder tincture have been shown to inhibit pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, reducing redness, swelling, and itching associated with dermatitis.

Antimicrobial properties: Elder tincture's antimicrobial activity helps combat bacterial and fungal infections that can contribute to or worsen dermatitis symptoms, promoting a healthy skin microbiome.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in soothing and calming skin irritations associated with dermatitis.
Rhyzomes: The rhizome of the elder plant is rich in bioactive compounds like alkaloids, which can help reduce inflammation and itching symptoms commonly experienced in dermatitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers at peak blooming stage for maximum potency and quality.
Clean and dry the harvested flowers thoroughly to prevent contamination and mold growth.
Chop the dried sambucus nigra flowers into small pieces and soak them in 40% vodka or grain alcohol for four weeks.
Shake the jar containing the herbal mixture daily for optimal extraction and transfer of active compounds during the four-week maceration period.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into dark glass bottles, discarding the solids, and store in a cool, dark place for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tinctures helps with dermatitis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which work to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with skin conditions. The tincture's soothing effects calm irritated skin, while its antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial or fungal infections that can exacerbate dermatitis. Additionally, plantain's natural moisturizing qualities hydrate the skin, reducing dryness and flakiness often seen in cases of eczema and other dermatological issues.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Plantain tincture helps reduce inflammation and swelling associated with dermatitis, relieving redness and discomfort.
Antimicrobial properties: Plantain's antimicrobial compounds combat bacterial and fungal infections that can exacerbate or cause dermatitis, promoting healing and preventing further infection.

Wound-healing properties: Plantain's tincture accelerates wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving skin elasticity, and reducing the appearance of scars and hyperpigmentation associated with dermatitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing skin inflammation and irritation associated with dermatitis.
Rhyzomes: Containing bioactive compounds that help soothe and calm irritated skin, while also promoting wound healing and reducing redness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantago major leaves and rinse them thoroughly with distilled water.
Chop the cleaned leaves into small pieces and soak them in 80% vodka for 4 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.
Discard the solids and store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least 2 months to allow maturation.
Dilute the mature tincture with distilled water to create a 20% plantago major extract using a 1:5 ratio.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for dermatitis are liquid extracts made from plants, used to soothe and calm skin irritations. They work by reducing inflammation and promoting healing, providing relief from itching, redness, and discomfort associated with dermatitis. Examples of herbal tinctures that help include aloe vera, chamomile, and lavender, which can be taken orally or applied topically. By using these tinctures, individuals suffering from dermatitis can experience improved quality of life, reduced anxiety, and increased confidence in their appearance, allowing them to participate fully in daily activities without discomfort or embarrassment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dermatitis that are treated with herbal tinctures are eczema, psoriasis, and acne. These conditions share similar symptoms such as inflammation, itching, and skin irritation. Herbal tinctures like tea tree oil, calendula, and lavender can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and inflammation. They can also be used to address underlying imbalances in the body that may contribute to these conditions, promoting overall health and well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with dermatitis is a blend of Calendula, Aloe Vera, and Chamomile. Calendula tincture promotes skin regeneration and reduces inflammation, while Aloe Vera soothes and calms the skin, reducing redness and itchiness. Chamomile tincture adds anti-inflammatory properties, calming the skin and reducing stress, which can contribute to dermatitis. Taking these tinctures internally and applying them topically can provide relief from symptoms and support the skin's natural healing process.
